title: [lichen_l2.c:160-165] LICHEN_FRAME_BASE_OVERHEAD excludes signature but includes in lora_l2.h
description:
Category not checked: Constant definition consistency

Bug found: There are TWO frame overhead calculations that don't align:

1. lichen_l2.c line 160-165:
   #define LICHEN_FRAME_BASE_OVERHEAD      (LICHEN_FRAME_LENGTH_FIELD + ... + LICHEN_FRAME_MIN_MIC)
   = 1 + 1 + 1 + 2 + 4 = 9 bytes (NO signature)

2. lora_l2.h line 70:
   #define LICHEN_LORA_FRAME_OVERHEAD 55
   = 5 (header) + 4 (MIC) + 48 (signature) - 2 (margin) = 55 bytes (WITH signature)

These constants are used for different purposes:
- LICHEN_FRAME_BASE_OVERHEAD: validates minimum frame size (9 bytes)
- LICHEN_LORA_FRAME_OVERHEAD: calculates MTU (255 - 55 = 200 bytes)

The naming is confusing:
- 'BASE_OVERHEAD' sounds like it should be the foundation
- 'FRAME_OVERHEAD' sounds like the same thing

Better names would be:
- LICHEN_FRAME_MIN_HEADER_SIZE (9 bytes, for validation)
- LICHEN_FRAME_MAX_OVERHEAD (57 bytes, for MTU calculation)

Impact: P4 - Naming confusion, could lead to incorrect use of constants.
close_reason: Fixed: Added clarifying comments distinguishing LICHEN_FRAME_BASE_OVERHEAD (9 bytes, for runt frame validation) from LICHEN_LORA_FRAME_OVERHEAD (55 bytes, conservative MTU overhead).
